title: call
html:
call

  • Last UpdatedDez 26, 2024
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

Execute the specified function or global ProcScript. 

call  {Library::}LitFunctionName { ( ArgumentList ) }

Example: call myFunction

Parameters

Table 1. Parameters
Parameter Data Type Description
Library Literal Library containing the global ProcScript module. If not specified, the default library is used.
LitFunctionName Literal Literal name of a module declared on a function statement; do not enclose LitFunctionName in double quotation marks (").
ArgumentList String Comma-separated list of arguments to the module. The number of arguments supplied must match the number and type of parameters defined with the params statement for the LitFunctionName. If the data type of an argument does not match the type of the corresponding parameter, Uniface attempts to convert the data to the proper type.

The ArgumentList is of the form:

Argument 1 , Argument 2 , ... , Argument n

Return Values

Table 2. Values returned in $status
Value Description

-1

An error occurred. $procerror contains the exact error.

Values of parameters with direction OUT and INOUT must be considered to be undefined in the component that called LitFunctionName.

0

$status has not been assigned a value, no value is returned by the called module, or there is no return statement present.
>0 Value returned by the function that was called. (Uniface expects -1 to be an error, so it is not a good idea to return that value from the called ProcScript function.)
Note: The value in $status can affect the way the structure editor behaves when it is activating a sequence of triggers. If you call a function in a trigger, make sure that the value returned by the function does not adversely affect the structure editor.
Table 3. Values returned in $procerror following call
Value Symbolic error Meaning

-1109

<UPROCERR_FUNCTION>

The function name specified cannot be found.

-1113

<UPROCERR_PARAMETER>

Parameter name not valid or not defined.

-1122

<UPROCERR_NARGUMENTS>

Wrong number of arguments.

Use

Allowed in all component types. However, calling a global ProcScript is not allowed in self-contained services and reports.

Description

In specifying the argument list:

  • When the function expects a basic parameter (that is, a field, component variable, or a named parameter) with direction IN, the corresponding argument in ArgumentList can be a constant, a field, an indirect reference to a field, a variable, or a function. The argument's value is placed in the module's parameter at the start of the function.
  • When the function expects a basic parameter with direction INOUT, the corresponding argument in ArgumentList can be a field, an indirect reference to a field, a variable, or a function to which a value can be assigned. The argument's value is placed in the module's parameter at the start of the function; the value in the function at its end is returned to the field, variable, or function.
  • When the function expects a basic parameter with direction OUT, the corresponding argument in ArgumentList can be a field, an indirect reference to a field a variable, or a function to which a value can be assigned. The value in the function at its end is returned to the field, variable, or function.

Locating the Called Module

When resolving a call statement, Uniface looks for a local function in the triggers of the component.

When resolving a call to a global ProcScript, and no library is specified by the call statement, Uniface looks for it in the following order:

  1. If no library is specified, in the component library
  2. In the library specified in the application shell properties
  3. In the library SYSTEM_LIBRARY

If a library is specified by the call statement and Uniface cannot find it in that location, it looks no further and returns an error.

For more information, see Compilation Process.

Example: Using call

The following example shows how to use the call statement:

function LSTORE
 store
 if ($status < 0)
 message "Store error!"
 rollback
else
 message "Store done."
 commit
endif
end ; LSTORE
trigger store
 call LSTORE
 return ($status)
end; store
trigger accept
 if ($formdbmod = 1)
 call LSTORE
 endif
 return ($status)
end; accept
Related Links
  • Entries

    Entries are legacy ProcScript modules defined by the entry ProcScript statement. They can be used as private functions, or they can be executed in response to a call statement.

  • Global ProcScripts

    Global ProcScripts are global objects that are located in a runtime library and can be called from any ProcScript in the application. They enable you to define common functionality for use by one or more applications.

  • ProcScript and Memory

    When a component is compiled, any ProcScript is compiled into a tokenized intermediate format. This intermediate format code is then interpreted by Uniface at run time. The Uniface runtime places the compiled ProcScript into memory when the component is loaded, and then interprets it, one statement at a time.
